elements.text: This is the chapter introduction. It sets the scene before the first Bible question. It should be engaging and draw the reader into the chapter theme. It can contain bold or italic text.
This is a bridge paragraph. It connects two sections of questions, provides context, or develops a theme. It can span multiple sentences and contains bold or italic text as needed.
This is the chapter closing summary. It draws together the key themes of the chapter and points forward. It should be encouraging and leave the reader with something clear to hold on to.

elements.passageText: <sup>16</sup>For this is the way God loved the world: he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

elements._comment: --- REPEAT ELEMENT (reference to a previously defined elementId) ---

elements._comment2: Subtype omitted: plain question with no header or reflection label
elements.question: What does this verse tell us about God's motivation in sending his Son?

elements._comment: Subtype 'standard': explicit standard question — functionally identical to no-subtype but named
elements.question: What does Paul mean by 'righteousness'?

deeper.label: Go deeper …
deeper.question: How does Paul's understanding of righteousness differ from the way the word is used in everyday speech today?

elements._comment: Subtype 'header': question with a visible section header above it
elements.question: What does this verse tell us about God's motivation in sending his Son?

elements._comment: Subtype 'reflection': personal reflection question, typically at end of chapter
elements.question: What is one thing from this chapter you want to take away and act on this week?

elements._comment2: Subtype 'misunderstanding': question shown first, answer revealed on tap. Answer stored separately in top-level qaCallouts array (keyed by elementId).
elements.question: What do you understand by the word grace? Think about it before tapping 'Find out'.

elements._comment: Subtype 'general': expandable callout with answer inline (not in qaCallouts). Used for long Bible readings, definitions, or supplementary content.
elements.question: It's worth reading the whole passage before you begin...
elements.answer:
1In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

2Now the earth was without shape and empty, and darkness was over the surface of the watery deep, but the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the water.



(NET - see context ↗)

elements.caption: An optional caption shown below the image.

elements.instruction: Choose the option that best reflects how well you agree or disagree with each statement.
statements: I have a clear understanding of what I believe.
I am currently growing in my faith.
I regularly spend time with God in prayer and Bible reading.
